idea.title: Tight Spot
idea.hook: Thread a car into impossibly tight parking spots — one scrape and you reset.
idea.hook_ru: Заведи машину в невозможно тесные парковки — одно касание, и всё сначала.
idea.prototype: Top-down PORTRAIT parking-precision game. A kinematic car (x, y, heading, speed) auto-creeps forward at a low CREEP_SPEED; the player holds the LEFT or RIGHT half of the screen (or Arrow / A,D) to steer while it rolls, releasing to coast straight. TWO inputs only. A hand-authored level pack of 14 escalating layouts (outer walls + interior partitions + parked obstacle cars + traffic cones + ONE glowing neon target bay with a parked heading). WIN = the car settles fully inside the target bay aligned within ANGLE_TOL/POS_TOL for SETTLE_SEC. LOSE = collision with any wall/obstacle/cone (oriented-rect SAT + point-segment + poly-circle tests) flips gs='CRASH', flashes/shakes, then after CRASH_RESET_SEC instantly retries the SAME level (forgiving, no game over). A 3/2/1 star rating rewards cleanliness: start at 3, every reset drops the ceiling, and a path longer than PATH_PAR x the straight-line distance costs a star. After the 14 authored levels gs='WIN' (campaign complete) and the game rolls into a daily-seeded endless generator (mulberry32 on the date) of ever tighter spots. Mobile-first portrait at 393x852, canvas fills viewport via Math.max (Yandex 1.6.2.1), MENU + in-game ? tutorial in EN+RU, GF.persist save, ads user-click only.
idea.mutation_of: none (NEW family for the portfolio — first parking/driving-precision game across 108 prior games)
idea.why_novel: The 108-game portfolio has ZERO parking or driving-precision games. The vehicle_crash archetype dominates today's live market scan (15 refs, weight 1389) across Yandex 'popular' (NSR: Уличные гонки 11k votes, Смертельный Спуск 122k votes) and CrazyGames 'new' (Jeep Parking 3D score 99, Nitro Racing Go), yet we have nothing in the racing_vehicle family. Tight Spot is a fresh, validated family rather than a remix of an existing game. Its verb is 'precision-thread under constant forward pressure' — the car never stops creeping, so the tension is positional/geometric, not a timer, and the no-blame instant retry drives the 'one more try' loop. This is distinct from our arcade-dodge games (you don't dodge incoming danger, you commit a single clean line into a fixed gap) and from our merge/idle/cleaner families.
idea.non_timer_pressure: collision-fail + a constant forward auto-creep (you cannot stop and think; the world keeps coming so you must commit a line) + a 3/2/1 star economy that punishes resets and wandering paths. No countdown timer is ever a lose trigger; attemptTimer is a flavor stat only.
idea.lose_condition: gs='CRASH' when collide()===true for 1 frame (car oriented-rect overlaps any wall segment within WALL_THICK, any obstacle car via SAT, or any cone via poly-circle). On crash: freeze + red flash + shake + crashes++, then after CRASH_RESET_SEC (0.65s) the SAME level resets to its start pose (gs='PLAYING'). Forgiving instant-retry, not a run-ending game over, but a real terminal transition out of PLAYING.

sources.name: vehicle_crash CTR archetype — live market scan 2026-05-27
sources.note: SIGNAL pick — outside-in from today's live CTR/market scan. The vehicle_crash archetype (15 refs, weight 1389) dominates Yandex 'popular' + CrazyGames 'new'. Daily-mix slots were empty and the opportunities/ideas files were missing today, so this is a straight outside-in read of the live scan. Cover uses the archetype: a glossy cartoon car nosing into a tight neon parking bay at a dramatic angle, two parked cars squeezing the gap, traffic cones, saturated foreground, high contrast, will-it-fit tension. CARTOON/abstract only (Yandex 5.6 + 8.3.6).

synthesizer_brief: SIGNAL-PICK (synthesis origin, outside-in from the live CTR/market scan). The bet: the vehicle_crash / racing_vehicle family is the single most dominant, most validated archetype in today's scan (15 refs, weight 1389 — leading Yandex 'popular' and CrazyGames 'new'), and our 108-game portfolio has ZERO entries in it. Rather than chase the 3D arcade-racer look (heavy, framework-y, off-brand for our vanilla-canvas pipeline), Tight Spot distils the family to its most teachable, most mobile-thumb-friendly core: parking precision. Three things make it ship-worthy: (1) ONE-GLANCE CLARITY — the glowing neon bay with a dashed ghost-car silhouette tells you exactly where and which way to end up, and the first level is a gentle straight-in so the mechanic teaches itself in 5s, with a full EN+RU MENU tutorial and a ? help button; (2) TWO-INPUT MOBILE-FIRST CONTROL — hold-left / hold-right screen halves with an auto-creep forward, so the entire game is one-thumb portrait at 393x852 (canvas fills via Math.max, touch-action:none); (3) NON-TIMER PRESSURE done right — the car never stops creeping (positional/geometric pressure, a held breath), collisions instantly but forgivingly reset the level, and a 3/2/1 star economy rewards clean short lines. Depth: 14 hand-authored layouts escalating through squeezes, cone chicanes, dog-leg walls and sideways bays, then a daily-seeded endless generator of ever tighter spots so there is always a next gap. Cover leans hard into the vehicle_crash CTR archetype: a glossy cartoon car nosing into an impossibly tight glowing bay between two parked cars with cones, dramatic rim light, high contrast — cartoon/abstract only per Yandex 5.6/8.3.6, no title text in the image.

alternatives.title: Shop Rush (idle_tycoon)
alternatives.note: Today's idle_tycoon archetype is strong too (Merge Dale #1 Yandex popular, 3,176 votes; Shop Rush 3D + Gourmet Empire on CrazyGames 'new'). A restock-the-shelves / serve-the-queue idle-tycoon loop with a visible reward pile. Rejected for THIS signal slot because idle_tycoon/merge is already well covered in our portfolio (multiple merge games) and the brief asked for a fresh family; parking/vehicle is the genuine portfolio gap. Strong KNOWLEDGE-slot candidate instead.

alternatives.title: Survivor Swarm (danger_combat / survivor.io)
alternatives.note: The danger_combat / survivor.io archetype (Swarm Survivor, Tower Core Survivors, Ball Battle Simulator on CrazyGames 'new') — auto-attacking hero vs encroaching swarm with upgrade picks. Deferred: we just shipped Parry Core (108) in the adjacent arcade-reflex/danger family on 2026-05-25, so a survivor clone would crowd that space; the vehicle gap is more valuable.

alternatives.title: Word Sweep (word/puzzle)
alternatives.note: From the day's digest direction — a word-search / letter-sweep puzzle (cf. Word Play on CrazyGames 'new'). A calm puzzle family. Deferred: word games lean text-heavy and need careful RU localisation of letter sets (Cyrillic), and the brief prioritised the dominant validated vehicle_crash signal for the SIGNAL slot. Held as a future KNOWLEDGE/puzzle pick.

alternatives.title: Parallel Park Master (vehicle_crash variant)
alternatives.note: A strict parallel-parking-only variant with a reverse gear and a steering-wheel dial. Rejected because reverse + a dial pushes past the 2-input mobile-first mandate and is far harder to teach in 5s than hold-left/hold-right with auto-creep. The sideways-bay levels (6, 10, 13) already give Tight Spot a taste of the parallel-park fantasy without the extra input.
